items.title: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. He's getting two years and $18 million unless both sides simultaneously opt out of it next June, which presumably would only happen if they had already agreed on his next contract. (1/2)
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Okay just got to the bottom of this -- Keon Ellis's deal is a true mutual option for year 2. He's getting two years and $18 million unless both sides simultaneously opt out of it next June, which presumably would only happen if they had already agreed on his next contract. (1/2)

items.title: Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Keon Ellis has agreed to a 2-year, $18 million deal with the Brooklyn Nets, league sources confirmed to @fredkatz.bsky.social and me. The contract has a team AND player option for Year 2, so either side can choose to opt in. 
Shams first on it.

Here is a flow chart for the deal:

items.title: Brooklyn Nets salary cap situation with Keon Ellis.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Brooklyn Nets salary cap situation with Keon Ellis.

Cap space is fluid in terms of how to procedurally sign him, Day'Ron Sharpe, and Josh Minott.

They would still have just under $35 million in cap space if they take Ellis and Minott into cap space & Sharpe into room mid-level.

items.title: BORD$ might overvalue Keon Ellis a bit but that's really strong value for Brooklyn to get him at that price, and he fills a clear need for a 3-and-D shooting guard as well.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt:
BORD$ might overvalue Keon Ellis a bit but that's really strong value for Brooklyn to get him at that price, and he fills a clear need for a 3-and-D shooting guard as well.
